Production-Ready AI-Assisted Web Application for a Vocal Studio

Multi-role booking, memberships, payments, notifications, admin controls, and analytics platform for a real service-based business.

Liza Yaroshchuk Data Scientist / AI & Data Product Builder Live website: kylymstudio.com April 2026
Core message

I designed, architected, and delivered a production-ready multi-role web application in a short timeframe, using AI tools as an accelerator while owning product logic, data modelling, workflow design, and delivery.

KYLYM Studio Case Study01 / 12
AI-assisted delivery and impact

AI accelerated execution; I owned the product logic and delivery

AI tools were used as an acceleration layer for planning, frontend and backend iteration, debugging, documentation and design refinement. The architecture, workflows, data model, business rules and quality decisions were owned by me.

1. DiscoveryBusiness rules, roles, memberships, policies and requirements.
2. MVPPublic site, auth, core user flows and initial booking journey.
3. LogicMemberships, scheduling constraints, blocked dates and admin settings.
4. OperationsPayments, notifications, logs and reliability improvements.
5. InsightsDashboard design, business metrics and production deployment.

Executive Summary03 / 12
Business problem

Before vs after: centralising studio operations

Before: fragmented manual process

  • Bookings managed through messages, calendars, or spreadsheets
  • Teacher availability and blocked dates tracked manually
  • Membership usage and remaining lessons difficult to monitor
  • Payments, cancellations and reschedules disconnected from operations
  • Limited visibility into revenue, cash flow and teacher utilisation

After: integrated digital workflow

  • Students, teachers and admins work through role-based flows
  • Booking logic respects schedules, memberships and restrictions
  • Memberships, lesson credits and payment statuses are tracked
  • Notifications improve reliability and reduce manual follow-ups
  • Analytics dashboard gives the business owner actionable visibility

Caption: Demonstrates how bookings, statuses, teachers and lesson types are managed inside one operational product flow.

Business Problem04 / 12
My role

End-to-end product and technical ownership

I worked closely with the business owner to clarify operational needs, translate business rules into product requirements, and design workflows that could support real studio operations. AI tools accelerated implementation, but the core product, architectural, analytical and quality decisions were owned by me.

Requirement discoveryProduct structureUser flowsData modelScheduling logicPayment flowsNotification workflowsDashboard designTesting & iterationStakeholder communication

Ownership highlights

1

Clarified business rules Converted studio policies into product logic: memberships, bookings, cancellation and rescheduling rules.

2

Designed the system model Structured entities for students, teachers, bookings, memberships, payments, settings and notifications.

3

Delivered a usable product Built and iterated a live application suitable for operational use and recruiter walkthroughs.

My Ownership05 / 12
Roles, modules and stack

Multi-role application with access control

RolePrimary permissions
StudentSign up, manage account, book lessons, use memberships, view booking status and receive confirmations.
TeacherView assigned lessons, manage availability context, work with studio schedule and booking flow.
AdminManage teachers, students, memberships, prices, schedules, blocked dates, payments, notifications and analytics.
Security and privacy note

The case study uses demo/blurred data for sharing. Credentials and real personal/payment details should never be included in the recruiter version.

Public siteMarketing, teachers, offer
AuthLogin and role routing
Student portalBookings and memberships
Teacher flowSchedule visibility
AdminOperational controls
Booking engineAvailability rules
PaymentsStatus and packages
NotificationsEmail and logs
Studio settingsPrices and policies
Blocked datesScheduling restrictions
AnalyticsRevenue and usage
DeploymentLive website
LovableSupabaseVercelStripeResendGoogle Calendar
RBAC + Stack06 / 12
Application architecture

Structured workflow from public website to business insight

Why this matters

  • Separates marketing content from logged-in operational workflows
  • Connects booking rights to membership status and payment context
  • Centralises business rules in admin settings rather than manual decisions
  • Creates a foundation for monitoring, analytics and future scaling

Caption: Shows the public-facing website navigation, multilingual interface, admin access and account entry points.

Application Architecture07 / 12
Data model and schema

Relational data model supporting real business workflows

The schema supports real-world relational logic: many students, many teachers, multiple membership types, bookings tied to schedules, payment status, blocked dates, notification logs, and admin-configurable studio settings.

Operational

  • Students
  • Teachers
  • Bookings
  • Teacher schedules
  • Blocked dates

Business

  • Membership types
  • Memberships
  • Payments
  • Prices
  • Cancellation policy

Reliability

  • Notifications
  • Notification logs
  • Delivery status
  • Error messages
  • Auditability

Insight

  • Revenue
  • Lesson usage
  • Teacher utilisation
  • Cancellations
  • Cash flow
Data Model08 / 12
Booking and scheduling logic

More than a calendar: rules-based booking engine

Core constraints handled by the product

  • Students can book only available time slots
  • Teacher schedules define recurring availability
  • Blocked dates prevent booking on unavailable days
  • Membership validity and remaining lesson credits affect booking rights
  • Cancellation and rescheduling flows follow policy logic
  • Invalid booking states are prevented rather than fixed manually later

Caption: Shows user-facing lesson planning with date/time slot selection, membership usage and payment continuation.

Caption: Shows admin control over recurring teacher availability, lesson duration, buffer time and active schedule windows.

Screenshot intentionally omitted

Caption: The case study keeps the scheduling restriction concept in the logic description, while avoiding adding an unrelated settings screenshot.

Booking Logic09 / 12
Memberships and payments

Monetisation workflows connected to booking rights

Membership logic

  • Supports single lessons and lesson packages
  • Handles 4-lesson and 8-lesson membership options
  • Tracks validity period, active status and remaining lessons
  • Connects membership status to whether a student can book
  • Allows admin visibility into package usage and payment context

Payment flow

Designed to track payment provider, payment status, amount, currency and membership association so the operational workflow can reflect paid, pending or failed states.

Caption: Shows how membership credits influence the booking journey and how the user continues to payment after planning lessons.

Caption: Demonstrates monetisation and payment-state tracking, including payment status, description and method fields.

Caption: Shows operational control over active memberships, credits, validity dates, paid amount and membership actions.

Memberships + Payments10 / 12
Notifications, reliability and privacy

Operational reliability built into communication flows

Notification workflows

  • Booking confirmations for students, teachers and admin
  • Cancellation notifications with policy-aware messaging
  • Rescheduling notifications to keep stakeholders aligned
  • Membership reminders before package completion or renewal points
  • Notification logs for delivery status, errors and debugging

Reliability design

Notifications were designed not only as messages, but as auditable events. Logs make the system easier to monitor, debug, and improve as business volume grows.

Access and data handling

Role-based access control separates student, teacher and admin capabilities. Sensitive personal and payment data should be hidden or replaced with demo records in external presentations.

No screenshot included

Caption: Notification logic is described in the case study, but no settings/policies screenshot is included in this HTML version.

Notifications + Reliability11 / 12
Analytical dashboard

Helping the owner manage revenue, demand and cash flow

The analytical dashboard was designed to help the business owner move from manual tracking to data-driven decision-making. It provides visibility into revenue, bookings, memberships and operational performance.

Revenue
Track income and cash flow trends
Demand
Identify popular time slots and teachers
Usage
Monitor lesson package consumption
Risk
Observe cancellations and no-show patterns
  • Improves visibility into business health
  • Supports pricing and schedule planning
  • Reduces dependency on manual spreadsheets

Caption: Shows revenue, bookings, memberships, cash-flow indicators and operational trends that support better business decisions.

Analytics Dashboard12 / 12
